steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Find two numbers that multiply to $-24$ and add to $10$. Those numbers are $12$ and $-2$.
$$z^2+10z-24=(z+12)(z-2)$$

steps.title: Find the roots
steps.content:
Set each factor equal to $0$.
$$z+12=0\quad\text{or}\quad z-2=0$$
$$z=-12\quad\text{or}\quad z=2$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing $y=x^2+10x-24$ shows the same two $x$-intercepts. Since the question asks for one solution, one valid entry is the positive root. The answer is 2.
